value:
v1.12.2 shipped 2026-04-16; v1.12.x integrity chain (#76-#80, #87) DONE.

v1.13.0 in development. Milestone has 14 open issues after 2026-04-23 cleanup (closed #92/#93, filed #94, assigned #89/#90 to milestone).

SHIPPED ON ORIGIN/MAIN (awaiting release tag):
- #92 MCP tool description audit — commit 530d284. 19 descriptions rewritten to explicitly disambiguate overlapping tool pairs. Core change: codex_get no longer advertises "list all entries" — drove the 48% empty-key pattern in the real-usage dataset. Acceptance signal: empty-codex_get rate should drop post-v1.13.0. Closed 2026-04-23.
- #93 audit log data quality gaps — commits 0e8f813 (Fix 1 + 3) and 745c5a3 (Fix 2). Three fixes: (1) 'remove' op is distinct from 'write' (classifyOp + TelemetryEntry/AuditEntry types); (2) bootstrapRate/writeBackRate labels now say "MCP sessions"; (3) CLI codex_run callsite now passes rawKey so aliasResolved gets captured. Closed 2026-04-23. Narrow follow-up #94 filed for codex_copy aliasResolved semantic.

STILL OPEN IN v1.13.0 (14):
- #91 formalize cross-session handoff protocol — context.next_session emerged as most-written key. Biggest conceptual change; design-first before coding.
- #94 codex_copy aliasResolved tracks dest instead of resolved source — narrow follow-up from #93 audit. No dataset evidence yet (no copy-with-alias calls in the 584-call window), filed preemptively.
- #88 askPassword non-interactive, #89 shape-invalid import bypass, #90 hardcoded 'ccli' hint — small bug fixes, can ship independently.
- #84 entry health, #82 seed-quality lint, #83 topology — Layer 2 tools (calibration data from dataset available).
- #86 CI bump (Node 20 sunset by Sept 2026).
- #45 attribution, #47 diff, #50 npx, #39 interpolation audit, #6 Fish/PowerShell completion — older items.

DOGFOODING: docs/dogfooding-real-usage.md committed 2026-04-22 as Part Two. Publishable alongside v1.13.0 tag. #92/#93 shipping validates the dataset-driven planning approach — worth a short addendum post.

NEXT: design pass on #91 (the interesting one). #88/#89/#90/#86/#94 are small cleanups that can ship anytime.
